title: My Old Bicycle
summary: An adult narrator in Amsterdam describes his old bicycle, where he rides it, and why he does not want a new one.
body: I have an old bicycle. Its name is Bertha. I bought Bertha eight years ago from a small shop near the canal. She was already old then. Her colour was once red, but now it is more pink. Her bell is loud. Her seat is soft. Her basket is big enough for my shopping. I ride Bertha every day. I ride to work in the morning. I ride to the market on Saturday. I ride to the park on Sunday with my dog in the basket. The streets in my city are flat. The wind from the sea is sometimes strong. Bertha is slow up small hills, but she is happy on the river path. Last spring her chain broke. I took her to a small repair shop. The man there fixed her in one day. He said, 'This bike is from another time.' I smiled. I do not want a new bicycle. My old bicycle knows the city. She knows me. We do not need anything new.

annotations.exampleEn: I ride my bicycle to school.
The bicycle bell is very loud.
The seat is soft and comfortable.
She put the bread in the basket.
The roads in this town are very flat.
The wind from the sea is cool.
There are small hills behind the town.
The chain came off my bike.
I took my watch to a repair shop.
His old jacket looks like it is from another time.

grammarNotes.title: Possessive 's and 'her' for things
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong tiếng Anh người ta thường gọi xe, thuyền hoặc thú cưng là 'she/her': 'Her bell is loud. Her seat is soft.' Đây là cách thân mật, không bắt buộc. Bạn cũng có thể dùng 'its'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: English speakers often call a beloved vehicle, boat or pet 'she/her': 'Her bell is loud. Her seat is soft.' It is friendly, not required — 'its' is also fine.

grammarNotes.title: Frequency phrases: every day, on Saturday, on Sunday
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi nói về thói quen ta dùng các cụm chỉ tần suất: 'I ride Bertha every day.' / 'I ride to the market on Saturday.' / 'I ride to the park on Sunday.' Lưu ý: 'on + thứ' (on Monday, on Friday), 'every day' không có 'on'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Frequency phrases describe habits: 'I ride Bertha every day.' / 'I ride to the market on Saturday.' / 'I ride to the park on Sunday.' Note: 'on' + day of week (on Monday), but no 'on' with 'every day'.

translationVi: Tôi có một chiếc xe đạp cũ. Nó tên là Bertha. Tôi mua Bertha cách đây tám năm từ một cửa hàng nhỏ gần con kênh. Lúc đó nó đã cũ rồi. Trước kia nó màu đỏ, nhưng giờ trông gần như màu hồng. Chuông của nó rất to. Yên xe êm. Giỏ xe đủ to để đựng đồ đi chợ. Tôi đi Bertha mỗi ngày. Sáng tôi đi làm. Thứ Bảy tôi đi chợ. Chủ nhật tôi đi công viên, với con chó trong giỏ xe. Đường trong thành phố tôi rất bằng phẳng. Gió biển đôi khi mạnh. Bertha lên dốc nhỏ thì chậm, nhưng chạy trên đường ven sông thì rất khoẻ. Mùa xuân năm ngoái, xích xe của nó đứt. Tôi mang đến một tiệm sửa nhỏ. Chú thợ sửa giúp tôi trong một ngày. Chú nói: 'Cái xe này là từ một thời khác.' Tôi cười. Tôi không muốn một chiếc xe đạp mới. Chiếc xe cũ của tôi quen thành phố này. Nó quen tôi. Chúng tôi không cần gì mới cả.

questions.prompt: When did the narrator buy Bertha?

options: A small shop near the canal

questions.prompt: Where does he ride on Saturday?
questions.explanation: 'I ride to the market on Saturday.'

questions.prompt: What does he take to the park on Sunday?
questions.hint: Find what is in the basket.

questions.prompt: What are the streets in his city like?
questions.explanation: 'The streets in my city are flat.'

questions.prompt: How long did the repair take?
questions.explanation: 'The man there fixed her in one day.'

questions.prompt: What did the repair man say?
options: This bike is from another time
You should buy a new bike

questions.prompt: Why does the narrator not want a new bicycle?
options: He cannot ride a new bike
Bertha knows the city and knows him
questions.explanation: 'My old bicycle knows the city. She knows me. We do not need anything new.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Some people keep an old thing for many years. Why is that? Is there an old thing in your home that has a story?
